meta.description: Sync from akave Go SDK v0.4.4: storage contract ABI new functions/errors, access manager bytecode update, gRPC NodeAPI removal, error constant additions, connection pool refactoring, and testrand Block() helper.

execution_order: CHANGE-1: Update private/ipc/contracts/storage.py with new ABI functions and errors — apply first
CHANGE-2: Update private/ipc/contracts/access_manager.py compiled bytecode — apply in parallel with CHANGE-1
CHANGE-3: Verify private/ipc/errors.py error map is complete — already mostly done, apply any gaps
CHANGE-4: Remove gRPC NodeAPI/StreamAPI (nodeapi.proto deleted upstream) — verify no equivalent exists
CHANGE-5: Verify PolicyFactory removal — check if policy_factory.py exists anywhere
CHANGE-6: Audit SDK connection pool refactoring impact on Python SDK client — apply last

changes.title: Update Storage contract bindings with new ABI functions and errors
changes.description: The Storage smart contract ABI was updated with five new view/transaction functions and five new custom error types. The Python SDK's storage.py (generated ABI binding, 105 KB) must be updated to include the new function call methods.
changes.current_state: private/ipc/contracts/storage.py exists (105 449 bytes). It contains auto-generated bindings for the Storage contract. It does NOT contain methods for: add_file_chunks, fill_chunk_blocks (batch variant), get_block_peers_of_chunk, get_buckets_by_ids_with_files, or get_file_index_by_id. The errors NoPeersForCID, NotEligibleToUpgrade, TooManyBlockCIDs, WrongAuthority, InvalidPeerIndex may or may not be present.
changes.code_snippet_before:
// Go storage.go (before) — functions did NOT include batch variants:
func (_Storage *StorageTransactor) FillChunkBlock(opts *bind.TransactOpts, bucketName string, fileName string, chunkCID []byte, blockIndex uint8, cid []byte, nodeID [32]byte) (*types.Transaction, error)

changes.title: Update AccessManager compiled bytecode
changes.description: The AccessManager contract was recompiled. The ABI is unchanged; only the compiled bytecode changed. This matters for contract deployment in tests.
changes.current_state: private/ipc/contracts/access_manager.py (7 904 bytes). Contains ABI and bytecode for AccessManager. The ABI structure is unchanged; only the bytecode hex string differs from v0.4.4.
changes.required_change: Obtain the updated AccessManager bytecode from the akave contracts repository at tag v0.4.4. In access_manager.py, replace the 'bytecode' (and 'deployedBytecode' if present) hex string with the new value. Do NOT change the ABI array.

changes.title: Verify and complete error selector map in private/ipc/errors.py
changes.description: Go's errors.go gained many new named error constants with EVM selectors plus a new IgnoreOffsetError helper. Python's private/ipc/errors.py already contains most of these, but should be audited for completeness.
changes.current_state:
private/ipc/errors.py already contains:
  "0xc4c1a0c5": "BucketNotFound",
  "0x3bcbb0de": "FileDoesNotExist",
  "0xa2c09fea": "NotThePolicyOwner",
  "0x94289054": "CloneArgumentsTooLong",
  "0x4ca249dc": "Create2EmptyBytecode",
  "0xf3714a9b": "ECDSAInvalidSignatureS",
  "0x367e2e27": "ECDSAInvalidSignatureLength",
  "0xf645eedf": "ECDSAInvalidSignature",
  "0xb73e95e1": "AlreadyWhitelisted",
  "0xe6c4247b": "InvalidAddress",
  "0x584a7938": "NotWhitelisted",
  "0x227bc153": "MathOverflowedMulDiv",
  "0xe7b199a6": "InvalidBlocksAmount",
  "0x59b452ef": "InvalidBlockIndex",
  "0x55cbc831": "LastChunkDuplicate",
  "0x2abde339": "FileNotExists",
  "0x48e0ed68": "NotSignedByBucketOwner",
  "0x923b8cbb": "NonceAlreadyUsed",
  "0x9605a010": "OffsetOutOfBounds".
  ignore_offset_error() function is already present.

MISSING from private/ipc/errors.py (present in Go v0.4.4 errors.go, not found above):
  "0x0d6b18f0": "NotBucketOwner" (selector from Go: ErrNotBucketOwner)
changes.required_change:
In private/ipc/errors.py, in the error_map dict inside error_hash_to_error(), add:
  "0x0d6b18f0": "NotBucketOwner"

Also add the following from the Go errors.go if not present:
  Storage contract new errors (verify these selectors are not already mapped by a different name):
  NoPeersForCID, NotEligibleToUpgrade, TooManyBlockCIDs, WrongAuthority, InvalidPeerIndex
  (If their selectors are already in the map under different names, leave them as-is.)

Note: Go's IgnoreOffsetError function is already ported as ignore_offset_error() in Python.
changes.code_snippet_before:
// private/ipc/errors.go — before v0.4.4: these entries were absent:
// ErrNotBucketOwner, ErrBucketNotFound, ErrFileDoesNotExist, etc.

changes.title: Remove gRPC NodeAPI / StreamAPI transport layer (nodeapi.proto deleted)
changes.description: Go deleted private/pb/nodeapi.proto (and generated files) — this was the streaming gRPC service (NodeAPI + StreamAPI) used for non-IPC file operations. The Python repo has private/pb/ipcnodeapi_pb2.py which is a DIFFERENT service (IPC-over-gRPC) and was NOT deleted. Verify no Python file references 'nodeapi' (not 'ipcnodeapi').
changes.current_state: private/pb/ in Python contains: __init__.py, ipcnodeapi.proto, ipcnodeapi_pb2.py, ipcnodeapi_pb2_grpc.py. There is NO nodeapi.proto or nodeapi_pb2.py. The Python SDK does not have the streaming NodeAPI service.
changes.required_change: Verify that private/pb/ contains ONLY ipcnodeapi-related files. If any nodeapi_pb2.py or nodeapi_pb2_grpc.py files are found, delete them. No action should be needed since the Python repo already uses only the IPC gRPC service.

changes.title: Remove PolicyFactory contract binding (deleted upstream)
changes.description: Go's PolicyFactory contract binding was deleted entirely. Verify the Python contracts directory has no equivalent file.
changes.current_state: private/ipc/contracts/ contains: __init__.py, access_manager.py, akave_token.py, erc1967_proxy.py, errors.py, list_policy.py, pdp_verifier.py, sink.py, storage.py. There is no policy_factory.py.
changes.required_change: If private/ipc/contracts/policy_factory.py exists, delete it and remove its import from __init__.py. Based on current directory listing, no action is needed.

changes.title: SDK connection pool promoted to struct field (internal refactor)
changes.description: In Go, the per-call connectionPool was promoted to an IPC struct field. Instead of creating a new pool per Upload/Download call and destroying it on return, the pool is now created once when the SDK is initialized (New()) and closed when Close() is called. This improves performance for long-lived SDK instances.
changes.current_state: Python SDK is structured differently. The akavesdk/akavesdk.py wraps the Go SDK via gRPC or direct calls. The connection management pattern in Python likely differs from Go.

changes.title: Add Block() test helper to testrand equivalent
changes.description: Go's testrand package gained a Block() function that generates a random IPFS Block with CIDv1/DagProtobuf/SHA2-256 CID and random data. This is used in integration tests.
changes.current_state: Python repo has tests/ directory and private/ipctest/ directory. No direct testrand equivalent was identified.
changes.required_change:
If Python integration tests need to generate random IPFS blocks, add a helper function:

  def random_block(size: int) -> tuple[bytes, str]:
      data = os.urandom(size)
      digest = hashlib.sha256(data).digest()
      mh = encode_multihash(digest, codec=SHA2_256)
      cid = CIDv1(dag_pb_codec, mh)
      return data, str(cid)

This is test-only code and optional.

contract_updates.encoding_notes: FillChunkBlockArgs.blockIndex is uint8. In web3.py, encode as Python int (0–255). Do NOT pass as a 32-byte padded value.

modified_functions.name: (bytecode only)
modified_functions.change_description: Recompiled bytecode. ABI is unchanged. Update the bytecode hex string in access_manager.py from the v0.4.4 contracts repository.

test_updates.description: Tests that operating on a non-existent bucket returns 'BucketNotFound' (previously 'failed to get bucket: not found'). The error is now decoded from EVM revert selector 0xc4c1a0c5.
test_updates.port_instructions:
In Python integration tests, update any assertion that checks for 'failed to get bucket: not found' or similar legacy messages. The new expected error string is 'BucketNotFound'. Use error_hash_to_error() from private/ipc/errors.py to decode the revert reason and assert the result equals 'BucketNotFound'.

Example:
  with pytest.raises(Exception) as exc_info:
      sdk.bucket_get("nonexistent-bucket")
  decoded = error_hash_to_error(exc_info.value)
  assert str(decoded) == "BucketNotFound"

test_updates.description: Tests that downloading a non-existent file returns 'BucketNotFound' or 'FileDoesNotExist' (previously 'file not exists').
test_updates.port_instructions: Update Python tests that assert 'file not exists'. The new error strings are 'FileDoesNotExist' (selector 0x3bcbb0de) or 'FileNotExists' (selector 0x2abde339) depending on which contract function reverts. Use error_hash_to_error() to decode and assert the correct name.
